title: Web roadmap: union layering, after field, and soft-edge badge in the board
description:
Bring the tickboard web roadmap to parity with the Go implementation (internal/query/roadmap.go, already shipped): waves layer on the UNION of epic-only blocked_by + after edges (deduped); the queued status consults hard (blocked_by) edges only; missing/non-epic targets ignored for both edge kinds; cycle flush unchanged over the union graph.

Context (verified):
- internal/tickboard/ui/src/stores/roadmap-compute.ts is the client-side mirror of ComputeRoadmap used by cloud boards (local boards use GET /api/roadmap, whose payload now already includes after). Its edge build at ~line 109-112 filters blocked_by to epicSet only; epicConsumerStatus at ~line 36-64.
- internal/tickboard/ui/src/api/ticks.ts ~line 304: manual interface RoadmapEpic lacks after — add: /** IDs of preferred-predecessor epics (soft ordering) — omitted when empty */ after?: string[]; (the generated types under src/types/generated/ were already refreshed and include after on Tick).
- internal/tickboard/ui/src/components/roadmap-view.ts renders badge chips: badge-blocked "⊘ <id>" per blocked_by entry (~line 458-460), styles ~line 300-331. Add a softer badge for after entries, e.g. "→ <id>", with a muted Catppuccin Mocha color (overlay/subtext tones — clearly weaker than the blocked chip; CSS variables are already in use, see var(--peach) usage).

Work:
1. roadmap-compute.ts: union layering (dedupe ids in both lists), hard-only queued, populate after on the returned RoadmapEpic (epic-only targets, omitted when empty).
2. api/ticks.ts: add after?: string[] to RoadmapEpic.
3. roadmap-view.ts: render after chips after blocked chips with the muted style.
4. Rebuild embedded assets: run scripts/build-ui.sh from the repo root and commit regenerated internal/tickboard/server/static/ and internal/tickboard/ui/dist/ if changed.

Test cases (extend internal/tickboard/ui/src/stores/roadmap-compute.test.ts, mirroring the Go table cases):
- B after A: B in wave 1, status ready (not queued), after == [A], blocked_by absent.
- B blocked_by A (open) and C after B: B queued, C ready, waves [A][B][C].
- Union cycle (A after B, B blocked_by A): both in final wave, terminates.
- after target that is a task or missing id: ignored for layering, absent from after.
- Dedupe: B blocked_by A and after A: in-degree counted once.

Project rules: pnpm only (never npm/yarn), run commands in internal/tickboard/ui. Pre-existing baseline test failures exist in the UI suite — run pnpm vitest run src/stores/roadmap-compute.test.ts src/components/ (or the closest targeted invocation) and compare failures against an untouched baseline rather than requiring a fully green global suite; tsc --noEmit must be clean.

Files likely touched: internal/tickboard/ui/src/stores/roadmap-compute.ts, src/stores/roadmap-compute.test.ts, src/api/ticks.ts, src/components/roadmap-view.ts, internal/tickboard/server/static/ (regenerated), internal/tickboard/ui/dist/ (regenerated).
Run: cd internal/tickboard/ui && pnpm exec tsc --noEmit && pnpm exec vitest run src/stores/roadmap-compute.test.ts; then scripts/build-ui.sh from repo root.
acceptance_criteria: roadmap-compute mirrors Go union layering with hard-only queued; after badges render distinct from blocked chips; pnpm test (roadmap files) + tsc --noEmit pass; embedded assets rebuilt and committed
